what: Compute High-Complexity Multi-Risk Scores and (Eco)system Risk — aggregate_risk
title: Compute High-Complexity Multi-Risk Scores and (Eco)system Risk — aggregate_risk
text: function aggregate_risk uses output risk function vulnerability function. risk vulnerability scores aggregated three ways: multi-pressure risk per state indicator, representing overall effect one indicator multi- state indicator risk per pressure, representing overall effect pressure state indicators ecosystem risk, combined multi-pressure risks indicators.

title: Compute High-Complexity Multi-Risk Scores and (Eco)system Risk — aggregate_risk
code: aggregate_risk(risk_results, method = "mean")

title: Compute High-Complexity Multi-Risk Scores and (Eco)system Risk — aggregate_risk
text: risk_results Risk score state indicator ~ pressure ~ type combination, derived function risk vulnerability. method Character indicating method aggregating pressures state indicators multiple risk scores ecosystem risk score. use can choose (arithmetic) mean, median, sum, maximum minimum. Default arithmetic mean.

title: Compute High-Complexity Multi-Risk Scores and (Eco)system Risk — aggregate_risk
text: function returns list containing three sublists, first multi-state indicator risk list, containing risks uncertainties aggregated per pressure, type pathway. Second multi-pressure risk list, risks uncertainties aggregated per state indicator type pathway. third list contains ecosystem risks, aggregates multi-pressure risk uncertainty scores per type pathway.

title: Compute High-Complexity Multi-Risk Scores and (Eco)system Risk — aggregate_risk
text: returned lists required plotting functions plot_radar plot_heatmap. aggregated scores calculated type pathway combination individually across types pathways. one type /one pathway evaluated beforehand, results different combinations.

what: Calculate Overall Exposure Scores from Component-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_exposure
title: Calculate Overall Exposure Scores from Component-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_exposure
text: Calculate exposure scores individual exposure components. Additionally calculate associated uncertainty scores.

title: Calculate Overall Exposure Scores from Component-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_exposure
code: calc_exposure(   pressures,   components,   probabilities = NULL,   uncertainty = NULL,   method = "mean" )

title: Calculate Overall Exposure Scores from Component-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_exposure
text: pressures character vector column data frame containing names pressures. components numeric vector data frame containing numeric values per exposure component pressure. order pressure vector come data frame. probabilities Optionally: numeric vector containing probabilities pressure (values 0 1); default NULL. order pressure vector. uncertainty numeric vector  data frame containing associated uncertainty per component; default NULL. order pressure vector. method character string specifying aggregation method. Available mean (default), median, maximum, minimum.

title: Calculate Overall Exposure Scores from Component-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_exposure
text: Returns data frame containing pressure name, aggregated exposure score associated uncertainty scores. results serve input vulnerability function. case uncertainty values provided, NAs returned uncertainty scores.

title: Calculate Overall Exposure Scores from Component-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_exposure
text: Often exposure components include magnitude change compared baseline conditions, frequency change, future trend spatial scale. components scored within ecorisk framework pressure experts scale 1 (low impact) 5 (high impact). express uncertainty process, experts can score associated uncertainty generally components one pressure component individually. uncertainty scored ecorisk framework scale 1 (low uncertainty) 3 (high uncertainty). Using exposure sensitivity scorings vulnerability calculated. Guidance scoring process can found : create_template_exposure vignette Gutte et al., 2025.

title: Calculate Overall Exposure Scores from Component-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_exposure
code: ### Example using demo data with five pressures, four components and their individual # uncertainties (probabilities are assumed to be 1): ex_expert_exposure #>      pressure magnitude frequency trend spatial uncertainty_magnitude #> 1 temperature         1         1     4       5                     1 #> 2    salinity         1         4     1       3                     2 #> 3      oxygen         1         1     1       2                     2 #> 4    nutrient         2         2     3       2                     1 #> 5     fishing         5         4     5       2                     3 #>   uncertainty_frequency uncertainty_trend uncertainty_spatial #> 1                     1                 3                   3 #> 2                     2                 2                   2 #> 3                     2                 3                   3 #> 4                     2                 2                   3 #> 5                     1                 2                   1  calc_exposure(   pressures = ex_expert_exposure$pressure,   components = ex_expert_exposure[ ,2:5],   uncertainty = ex_expert_exposure[ ,6:9]  ) #>      pressure exposure uncertainty #> 1 temperature     2.75        2.00 #> 2    salinity     2.25        2.00 #> 3      oxygen     1.25        2.50 #> 4    nutrient     2.25        2.00 #> 5     fishing     4.00        1.75   ### Example for two hazardous risks with only two components ('magnitude' and #   'spatial'), one general uncertainty score, and associated probabilities: hazard <- c("heat waves", "hurricanes")  # Create scoring table using the template function: exp_tbl <- create_template_exposure(   pressures = hazard,   n_components = 2,   mode_uncertainty = "general",   probability = TRUE )  names(exp_tbl)[2:3] <- c("magnitude", "spatial") # Assign component-specific scores and probabilities: exp_tbl$magnitude <- c(5,4) exp_tbl$spatial <- c(5,3) exp_tbl$uncertainty <- c(2,3) exp_tbl$probability <- c(0.8,0.3)  # Calculate exposure score: calc_exposure(   pressures = exp_tbl$pressure,   components = exp_tbl[ ,c("magnitude", "spatial")],   probabilities = exp_tbl$probability,   uncertainty = exp_tbl$uncertainty  ) #>     pressure exposure uncertainty #> 1 heat waves     4.00           2 #> 2 hurricanes     1.05           3

what: Calculate Overall Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scores from Trait-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_sensitivity
title: Calculate Overall Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scores from Trait-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_sensitivity
text: function calc_sensitivity calculates aggregated sensitivity adaptive capacity scores. Additionally prepares scoring data usage vulnerability function. scores sensitivity adaptive capacity can trait-based general (one score per state indicator pressure combination).

title: Calculate Overall Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scores from Trait-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_sensitivity
code: calc_sensitivity(   indicators,   pressures,   type = "direct",   sensitivity_traits,   adaptive_capacities = NULL,   uncertainty_sens = NULL,   uncertainty_ac = NULL,   method = "mean" )

title: Calculate Overall Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scores from Trait-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_sensitivity
text: indicators character vector column data frame containing names state indicators. pressures character vector column data frame containing names pressures. type character vector column data frame specifying effect type. Effects direct indirect combination . Default direct. sensitivity_traits data frame containing numeric sensitivity values per species trait general value. adaptive_capacities data frame (single vector) containing numeric values adaptive capacity. Either per trait one general value. values given per trait, order values sensitivity. default NULL. uncertainty_sens data frame (vector) containing numeric uncertainty values associated sensitivity; default NULL. uncertainty_ac data frame (vector) containing numeric uncertainty values associated adaptive capacity; default NULL. method character string specifying method aggregation traits. Available median, minimum, maximum mean, mean default.

title: Calculate Overall Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scores from Trait-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_sensitivity
text: data frame containing indicator, pressure effect type, aggregated sensitivity adaptive capacity well associated uncertainty scores. Additionally, trait specific sensitivity adaptive capacity scores stored used later input vulnerability function.

title: Calculate Overall Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scores from Trait-Specific Expert Ratings — calc_sensitivity
text: function calculates per state indicator pressure combination one aggregated sensitivity adaptive capacity score, aggregation method can determined parameter method. assessment adaptive capacity optionally, scores adaptive capacity provided function calculates aggregated sensitivity score prepares sensitivity scores vulnerability function. Guidance scoring process can found : create_template_sensitivity vignette Gutte et al., 2025. Using exposure sensitivity scorings, vulnerability calculated.

what: Create a Template for Expert-Based Exposure Scoring — create_template_exposure
title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Exposure Scoring — create_template_exposure
text: function create_template_exposure generates template semi-quantitative, expert-based scoring individual exposure components. template designed use within ecorisk workflow. user can define number exposure components (e.g., magnitude, frequency, trend, spatial scale) included scoring process whether uncertainty assessed.

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Exposure Scoring — create_template_exposure
code: create_template_exposure(   pressures,   n_components = 4,   uncertainty = TRUE,   mode_uncertainty = "general",   probability = FALSE )

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Exposure Scoring — create_template_exposure
text: pressures character vector specifying names pressures assessed. n_components positive integer integer indicating number exposure components included assessment table. Within ecorisk framework four components usually scored:magnitude, frequency, trend spatial scale. default includes therefore four components. uncertainty logical; uncertainty assessed? Default TRUE. mode_uncertainty character; uncertainty assessed, can scored exposure component pressure individually ("component") general uncertainty score pressure ("general", default). default assigns one general uncertainty score per pressure. probability logical; hazardous risks may lower probability occurring within assessed future time period, probabilities 0 1 can assigned pressure considered calc_exposure function.

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Exposure Scoring — create_template_exposure
text: function returns data frame containing names pressures specified number exposure components. uncertainty assessed, data frame also includes either one general uncertainty column one uncertainty column per component, depending selected uncertainty assessment mode.

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Exposure Scoring — create_template_exposure
text: default, function creates template four exposure components general uncertainty scoring column. Within ecorisk framework, exposure components scored scale 1 5 (low high impact), uncertainty scored 1 3 (low high uncertainty). returned data frame can exported CSV Excel file. Components can renamed additional components can added necessary. completed file can analyzed using calc_exposure function. default scoring system follows: Exposure components: Scale 1 5 (low high impact). Uncertainty: Scale 1 3 (low high uncertainty).

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Exposure Scoring — create_template_exposure
code: ### Create a full template for three pressures, including all four components #   and a general uncertainty column: press <- c("fishing", "temperature increase", "salinity decrease")  exp_tbl <- create_template_exposure(pressures = press) # --> Export table and re-import after completion or fill in directly in R.  # Rename exposure components names(exp_tbl)[2:5] <- c("magnitude", "frequency", "trend", "spatial")  # Assign individual scores between 1 (low) and 5 (high impact): exp_tbl$magnitude <- c(1,5,4) exp_tbl$frequency <- c(1,5,3) exp_tbl$trend <- c(1,4,2) exp_tbl$spatial <- c(1,5,5)  # Assign uncertainty scores from 1 (low) to 3 (high uncertainty): exp_tbl$uncertainty <- c(1,1,3)   ### Create a template for two more hazardous risks with only two components #   ('magnitude' and 'spatial'), including component-specific uncertainties, #   and a probability column: hazard <- c("heat waves", "hurricanes")  exp_tbl <- create_template_exposure(   pressures = hazard,   n_components = 2,   mode_uncertainty = "component",   probability = TRUE )  # Rename components and uncertainties names(exp_tbl)[2:5] <- c("magnitude", "spatial", "uncertainty_magnitude",                          "uncertainty_spatial") exp_tbl$magnitude <- c(5,4) exp_tbl$spatial <- c(5,3) exp_tbl$uncertainty_magnitude <- c(1,1) exp_tbl$uncertainty_spatial <- c(3,3)  # Assign probabilities of their occurrence within the assessed future period: exp_tbl$probability <- c(0.8,0.3)

what: Create a Template for Expert-Based Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scoring — create_template_sensitivity
title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scoring — create_template_sensitivity
text: function crt_sensitivity creates template semi-quantitative, expert-based sensitivity , optionally, adaptive capacity scoring. template allows assessing sensitivity adaptive capacity using either general score state indicator-pressure combination trait-based approach using life history traits. latter particularly useful state indicators represent individual species detailed biological information available.

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scoring — create_template_sensitivity
code: create_template_sensitivity(   indicators,   pressures,   type = "direct",   n_sensitivity_traits = 1,   adaptive_capacity = TRUE,   mode_adaptive_capacity = "general",   uncertainty = TRUE,   mode_uncertainty = "general" )

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scoring — create_template_sensitivity
text: indicators character vector specifying names state indicators assess. pressures character vector specifying names pressures assess. type character vector defining type(s) influence, "direct", "indirect" "direct_indirect". default "direct". n_sensitivity_traits positive integer specifying number traits used assess sensitivity. default 1, meaning one general sensitivity score per state indicator-pressure-type combination provided. adaptive_capacity logical; adaptive capacity assessed? Default TRUE. mode_adaptive_capacity character vector specifying whether adaptive capacity assessed trait individually ("trait") general score ("general", default). Note: parameter relevant n_sensitivity_traits > 1. uncertainty logical; uncertainty assessed? Default TRUE. Note: Uncertainty added components included assessment. adaptive capacity (adaptive_capacity = FALSE) included, uncertainty scoring applied . mode_uncertainty character vector specifying whether uncertainty assessed trait individually ("trait") general score ("general").

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scoring — create_template_sensitivity
text: data frame row represents state indicator-pressure-type combination, containing specified sensitivity traits, adaptive capacity, uncertainty columns (selected). adaptive capacity uncertainty assessed, data frame includes either one general column one column per trait, depending settings. using trait-based scoring, data frame includes trait-specific sensitivity, adaptive capacity, uncertainty columns, can renamed needed.

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scoring — create_template_sensitivity
text: state indicator-pressure combination, different types influence can assessed. type influence describes whether pressure acts directly, indirectly, combination , important identifying impact pathways potential management measures. Within ecorisk framework, recommended use negative scores (-1 -5) indicate negative impacts (low high severity) positive scores (1 5) positive effects pressure indicator. indicator sensitive pressure, score 0. Adaptive capacity scored -1 (adaptive capacity) 1 (high adaptive capacity). improve reliability scoring, uncertainty also assessed. Uncertainty can scored trait individually general score rated scale 1 3 (low high uncertainty). returned data frame can exported CSV Excel file. Column names can modified needed. completed file can analyzed using calc_sensitivity function. Depending settings, data frame include: single "sensitivity" column, using general scoring. Multiple trait-specific sensitivity columns (e.g., "sensitivity_trait_1", "sensitivity_trait_2", etc.), can renamed necessary. Within data frame, trait-based general scoring can mixed. therefore recommended set n_sensitivity_traits maximum number traits assessed state indicator. calc_sensitivity function automatically distinguishes general trait-based scoring.

title: Create a Template for Expert-Based Sensitivity and Adaptive Capacity Scoring — create_template_sensitivity
code: ### Create a table for two state indicators and two pressures to evaluate direct #   effects (default). Return a general sensitivity and adaptive capacity #   column as well as uncertainty columns for both components: ind <- c("seabirds", "seals") press <- c("plastic pollution", "temperature increase")  sens_ac_tbl <- create_template_sensitivity(   indicators = ind,   pressures = press ) # --> Export table and re-import after completion or fill in directly in R.  # Assign sensitivity scores from -5 (strong negative response to pressure) # to +5 (strong positive response) (0 = no sensitivity): sens_ac_tbl$sens_general <- c(-5,3,-4,4)  # Assign adaptive capacity scores from -1 (none) to +1 (good adaptive capacity): sens_ac_tbl$ac_general <- c(-1,1,-1,1)  # Assign uncertainty scores from 1 (low) to 3 (high uncertainty): sens_ac_tbl$uncertainty_sens <- c(1,2,1,1) sens_ac_tbl$uncertainty_ac <- c(3,2,3,2)   ### Create a table for four indicators and three pressures to evaluate both direct #   and indirect effects.
